title: Acoustics Wave Interference
description: A 2D wave interference visualizer using direct pixel-buffer rendering, two-point-source superposition (sin(k·r₁ - ω·t) + sin(k·r₂ - ω·t)), neon cyan/magenta fringe color mapping, and interactive source repositioning.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Direct pixel-buffer rendering: ctx.createImageData → Uint8ClampedArray mutation → ctx.putImageData, bypassing shape drawing for maximum throughput
Two-source superposition: Y = sin(k·r₁ - ω·t) + sin(k·r₂ - ω·t) computed per pixel, with Euclidean distance to each emitter
Neon fringe color palette: Y > 0 interpolates from background (#030509) to cyan (#00f0ff); Y < 0 interpolates to magenta (#ff2a5f); Y ≈ 0 renders as dark charcoal node
Resolution scaling slider: step values 1-6 control internal canvas division (W/step × H/step), image-rendering: pixelated for crisp downsampled preview
Click-to-reposition: alternates activeSource (1↔2) on each click, maps canvas-relative coordinates to internal pixel grid for precision
Separation slider: dynamically recalculates emitter positions symmetrically around center, divided by step for correct internal coordinate spacing
Telemetry sampling: ~1% of pixels (every 100th) sampled per frame for peak amplitude average, constructive ratio (|Y| > 1), and phase velocity (ω/k)
Pause/Resume: toggles t accumulation, maintains render loop for frozen inspection of interference pattern
Reset vectors: zeroes t, un-pauses, recalculates emitter positions from current separation value
